title: carex oederi subsp. nevadensis
intro: Carex oederi subsp. nevadensis, also known as Nevada sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing infection. The health benefits of Nevada sedge include its potential to aid in respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its ability to support digestive health. Herbal preparations of Nevada sedge typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and roots, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or poultices.
